commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=26ce8bc4b6d2a60b1e2e7660110aeb0a989ce496)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` deadline terminalization in `src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts` and a registry regression test for plain `agent.wait` timeouts.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +59. Total +86 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main retries a plain `agent.wait` timeout when the child session row is still running, which matches the linked issue's stuck-active report. I did not run a live provider/channel repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should gate the missing real behavior proof, CI completion, and any maintainer override for the internal lifecycle proof gap.

target.title: fix(agents): enforce subagent run timeouts
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `sessions_spawn` accepted `runTimeoutSeconds`, but the subagent registry could keep a child run active forever when `agent.wait` returned a plain timeout without terminal snapshot fields.
- Why this matters now: #87444 reports parent sessions parked behind timed-out children, which can block completion delivery and consume active subagent capacity.
- Intended outcome: explicit subagent run timeouts now produce a terminal timeout outcome at the stored deadline, wake the parent path, and let normal cleanup/final delivery proceed.
- Intentionally out of scope: manual kill/reap behavior is left to #87445; this PR only handles the configured run timeout deadline.
- Success looks like: a child remains active before its deadline, then records `status: "timeout"` at the deadline even if gateway has not produced a terminal snapshot.
- Reviewer focus: the `agent.wait` timeout branch in `src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts` and the regression test in `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #87444

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87445

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested through the maintainer workflow for #87444.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_spawn` child runs with explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` no longer remain active after the deadline when `agent.wait` only reports a non-terminal timeout.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree using the repo Vitest wrapper on the final rebased commit; no live channel/provider credentials were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`; `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output from the final runs: `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 70 passed (70)`; `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts` reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 16 passed (16)`; `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 254 passed (254)`; `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD` produced no output.
- Observed result after fix: the new registry regression keeps the child active before the deadline, advances past the explicit timeout, then records `status: "timeout"`, `endedAt` at the exact stored deadline, `elapsedMs: 1000`, and one announce cleanup flow.
- What was not tested: live end-to-end channel/provider behavior, GitHub Actions CI, and the separate manual kill/reap path tracked by #87445.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is focused seam proof for the reported registry/gateway timeout shape; a live provider run was not executed because the fix is internal lifecycle bookkeeping and the regression is covered by injected `agent.wait` and session-store state.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the runtime fix, the added regression failed because `completedRun?.endedAt` stayed `undefined` instead of the expected timeout deadline, matching the stuck-active behavior from #87444.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added a subagent registry regression for the exact non-terminal wait timeout shape: gateway returns `{ status: "timeout" }`, the child session row remains `running`, and explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` has elapsed.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new regression failed before the production change because the child run stayed active and `endedAt` remained `undefined` after the explicit timeout deadline.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A; a focused regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prematurely marking a still-running child as timed out if the gateway wait path returns a plain timeout before the configured child deadline.

How is that risk mitigated?

The new branch only completes plain wait timeouts when `runTimeoutSeconds` is finite and positive and the current time is at or beyond the stored run deadline. Existing session-store reconciliation still wins first, and the existing retry behavior remains unchanged before the deadline.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub CI and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI has not completed yet; live channel/provider proof was not run and can be added if maintainers want end-to-end evidence beyond the focused lifecycle tests.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed maintainer feedback to use the title convention and restore the full PR template real-behavior proof fields.
